glazes.name: Obsidian
glazes.description: deep, opaque black glaze that pools and accents textured ware like ancient glazes

glazes.name: Smoke
glazes.description: Light transparent gray glaze that pools and accents textured ware, imitating ancient glazes. Opaque finish.

glazes.name: Charcoal
glazes.description: Deep translucent gray glaze that pools and accents textured ware like ancient glazes. Opaque.

glazes.name: Snow
glazes.description: A glossy, opaque white celadon glaze for tinting other AMACO Celadon glazes.

glazes.name: Mixing Clear
glazes.description: Mixing Clear is a stiff gloss zinc-free glaze that works well with most underglazes. Some underglazes may appear muted, including V-315 Peach, V-316 Light Pink, and V-323 Salmon.

glazes.name: Glacier
glazes.description: Pale transparent blue-green glaze pools and accents textured ware, imitating ancient glazes. Opaque with a glossy finish.

glazes.name: Cobalt
glazes.description: Deep, glossy cobalt blue glaze that pools and accents textured ware like ancient glazes. Opaque and translucent with a surface finish that mimics historical ceramics.

glazes.name: Sky
glazes.description: lively, glossy sky blue that pools and accents textured ware like ancient glazes

glazes.name: Fog
glazes.description: dark, glossy blue-gray that pools and accents textured ware like the ancient glazes it is created to imitate

glazes.name: Ice
glazes.description: Glossy, ice blue celadon glaze that pools darker blue and accents textured ware, imitating ancient glazes. Opaque and translucent.

glazes.name: Succulent
glazes.description: Translucent spearmint green - fresh and spring-like.

glazes.name: Downpour
glazes.description: Transparent indigo blue glaze that pools and accents textured ware, imitating ancient glazes. Opaque.

glazes.name: Lagoon
glazes.description: translucent aqua-tinted blue like a tranquil inlet

glazes.name: Storm
glazes.description: Deep, glossy navy that pools and accents textured ware like ancient glazes. Opaque.

glazes.name: Deep Sea
glazes.description: Cool transparent blue glaze that pools and accents textured ware, resembling ancient glazes. Opaque surface.

glazes.name: Ochre
glazes.description: Light amber brown glaze with pooling effects, accenting textured ware in an ancient glazed style.

glazes.name: Sandalwood
glazes.description: semi-translucent warm light brown like a sandy dune

glazes.name: Iron
glazes.description: Glossy medium brown celadon glaze that pools darker brown and accents textured ware, imitating ancient glazes. Opaque with a translucency that highlights the texture.

glazes.name: Aqua
glazes.description: Light, glossy blue-green that pools darker turquoise and accents textured ware like ancient glazes. Opaque with a translucency that mimics historic celadon finishes.

glazes.name: Pear
glazes.description: Bright, glossy yellow-green that pools and accents textured ware like ancient glazes. Opaque.

glazes.name: Saguaro
glazes.description: Translucent pale soft green like a tall cactus.

glazes.name: Wasabi
glazes.description: soft, glossy medium green that pools and accents textured ware like the ancient glazes it is created to imitate

glazes.name: Jade
glazes.description: Cool, glossy emerald green that pools and accents textured ware like ancient glazes.

glazes.name: Rainforest
glazes.description: Deep, glossy forest green pools and accents textured ware like ancient glazes. Opaque and imitative of historical celadon finishes.

glazes.name: Cherry Blossom
glazes.description: Glossy, soft, blushing pink glaze that pools and accents textured ware like ancient glazes, creating an opaque appearance.

glazes.name: Red Rock
glazes.description: semi-translucent deep russet brown like a Southwestern mesa

glazes.name: Weeping Plum
glazes.description: soft, glossy raspberry red that pools and accents textured ware like ancient glazes

glazes.name: Snapdragon
glazes.description: Bright, glossy red glaze that accents textured ware, imitating ancient glazes. Opaque and translucent with pooling and breaking effects.

glazes.name: Poppy
glazes.description: light red glaze pools and accents textured ware like ancient glazes

glazes.name: Lavender
glazes.description: Cool, glossy lavender purple that pools and accents textured ware like ancient glazes. Opaque.

glazes.name: Mulberry
glazes.description: Deep, glossy purple that pools and accents textured ware like ancient glazes. Opaque.

glazes.name: Marigold
glazes.description: A glossy, deep yellow glaze that pools and accents textured ware like ancient glazes. Opaque.

glazes.name: Tangelo
glazes.description: Transparent orange glaze pools and accents textured ware, imitating ancient celadon glazes. Opaque finish.

glazes.name: Supernova
glazes.description: Creamy white translucent glaze with a crystalline surface like frost on a window. Glossy, opalescent surface that breaks slightly more cream than white.

glazes.name: Solar Flare
glazes.description: Solar Flare is a highly fluid light brown glaze with an iridescent crystalline surface, opaque and glossy with a slight texture on the crystals.

glazes.name: Dark Star
glazes.description: Dark Star is a fluid, dark matte glaze with iridescent crystals like a beetle shell and breaks translucent.

glazes.name: Solar Rainbow
glazes.description: Solar Rainbow is a highly fluid, dark gloss glaze with iridescent rainbow crystals and breaks translucent.

glazes.name: Black

images.alt: Image of CR-1 Black on a No.11 A-Mix White Stoneware tile fired to cone 5.

glazes.description: CR-1 is a glossy black glaze that crawls into raised shapes of varying sizes and forms, creating unpredictable, natural patterns on the surface. The result adds unique texture and visual interest to each piece. Increased coats create larger glaze dots, while thinner coats produce a finer bead effect. More cracks develop with more coats.

glazes.name: Speckled White

images.alt: Image of CR-12 Speckled White on a No.30 Milk Chocolate tile fired to cone 5.

glazes.description: CR-12 is a glossy white glaze with black specks that develops unpredictable, natural patterns when fired, creating unique texture and visual interest. The surface features raised shapes of varying sizes and forms. More coats create larger glaze dots, while thinner coats produce a finer bead effect.

glazes.name: Flux Crawl

images.alt: Image of CR-17 Flux on a No.11 A-Mix White Stoneware tile fired to cone 5.

glazes.description: CR-17 is a glossy blush glaze that develops unique, multidimensional effects with subtle depth when fired, crawling into organic shapes and adding color variation to the underlying glaze. The result features a variety of crackle patterns and showcases an ancient glaze's beauty on AMACO No.11 A-Mix White Stoneware.

glazes.name: Speckled Flux Crawl

images.alt: Image of CR-18 Speckled Flux on a No.11 A-Mix White Stoneware tile fired to cone 5.

glazes.description: CR-18 is a glossy blush glaze with black specks that develops organic shapes and adds color variation when fired, creating a unique, multidimensional effect with subtle depth. Applied on AMACO No.11 A-Mix White Stoneware, more coats create larger glaze dots, while thinner coats produce a finer bead effect.

glazes.name: Dark Blue

images.alt: Image of CR-20 Dark Blue on a No.11 A-Mix White Stoneware tile fired to cone 5.

glazes.description: CR-20 is a glossy dark blue glaze that, when fired, creates unpredictable, natural patterns through crawling into raised shapes of varying sizes, adding unique texture and visual interest to each piece.

glazes.name: Speckled Teal

images.alt: Image of CR-22 Teal on a No.11 A-Mix White Stoneware tile fired to cone 5.

glazes.description: CR-22 is a glossy teal glaze with black specks that develops unpredictable, natural patterns when fired, adding unique texture and visual interest to each piece. The surface marked by crawling into raised shapes of varying sizes forms an ancient glaze-like appearance. Opaque and crawly, it features hairline cracks as it dries.

glazes.name: Lime

images.alt: Image of CR-41 Lime on a No.11 A-Mix White Stoneware tile fired to cone 5.

glazes.description: CR-41 is a glossy lime-green glaze that, when fired, creates raised shapes of varying sizes and forms, resulting in a surface marked by unpredictable, natural patterns adding unique texture and visual interest to each piece. Increased coats will produce more cracks.

glazes.name: Speckled Pink

images.alt: Image of CR-51 Speckled Pink on a No.11 A-Mix White Stoneware tile fired to cone 5.

glazes.description: CR-51 is a glossy pink glaze with black specks that, when fired, creates raised shapes of varying sizes and forms, resulting in a surface marked by unpredictable, natural patterns adding unique texture and visual interest to each piece.

glazes.name: Purple

images.alt: Image of CR-54 Purple on a No.11 A-Mix White Stoneware tile fired to cone 5.

glazes.description: CR-54 is a glossy purple glaze that crawls into raised shapes of varying sizes and forms, creating unpredictable, natural patterns on the surface. This adds unique texture and visual interest to each piece. More coats create larger glaze dots, while thinner coats produce a finer bead effect. Increased coats will produce more cracks.

glazes.name: Red

images.alt: Image of CR-58 Red on a No.11 A-Mix White Stoneware tile fired to cone 5.

glazes.description: CR-58 is a glossy red glaze that, when fired, crawls into raised shapes of varying sizes and forms, creating a surface marked by unpredictable, natural patterns adding unique texture and visual interest to each piece.

glazes.name: Speckled Yellow

images.alt: Image of CR-61 Speckled Yellow on a No.11 A-Mix White Stoneware tile fired to cone 5.

glazes.description: CR-61 is a glossy yellow glaze with black specks that develops unpredictable, natural patterns when fired, creating raised shapes of varying sizes and forms. The result adds unique texture and visual interest to each piece. The featured chip image showcases an ancient glaze effect on AMACO No.11 A-Mix White Stoneware.

glazes.name: Black
glazes.description: HF-1 Black is a glossy, opaque black glaze suitable for firing at cone 5 and above.

glazes.name: Zinc-Free Clear
glazes.description: Zinc-Free Clear is a glossy glaze that enhances the true colors of underglazes, stains, and coloring oxides. Some colors may appear muted with this glaze. V-315 Peach, V-316 Light Pink, and V-323 Salmon are among the shades that can be affected.

glazes.name: Clear
glazes.description: HF-10 Clear is a shiny bright glaze with zinc for hardness, suitable as a liner glaze or for use with cadmium underglazes. Works well with Bright Red, Radiant Red, Flame Orange, Bright Orange, and Intense Yellow. May shift colors when used with Warm Gray, Blue Green, Dark Green, Avocado, Teddy Bear Brown, and Hunter Green.

glazes.name: White
glazes.description: White is a glossy opaque white glaze.

glazes.name: Turquoise
glazes.description: HF Turquoise is a stiff, glossy, opaque light aqua glaze inspired by classic Fiesta tableware ceramics.

glazes.name: China Blue
glazes.description: Ancient glazed look, stiff, glossy, opaque medium blue.

glazes.name: Baby Blue
glazes.description: Baby Blue is a stiff, glossy, sky blue glaze inspired by classic Fiesta tableware ceramics, offering an ancient glazed effect and opacity.

glazes.name: Chartreuse
glazes.description: Chartreuse is a stiff, glossy, opaque lime green glaze.

glazes.name: Bright Yellow
glazes.description: Bright Yellow is an opaque, lemon colored glaze with a glossy finish, inspired by classic Fiesta tableware ceramics.

glazes.name: Scarlet Red
glazes.description: Scarlet Red is an opaque, crimson glaze that provides a consistent, reliable result suitable for classrooms. This glaze is food-safe and can be applied by spray. It works well as both a liner and a base for layering other glazes. Scarlet Red is appropriate for firing at cone 5 and above.

glazes.name: Orangerie
glazes.description: Orangerie is an opaque, yellow-orange glaze with a consistent result. Suitable for firing from cone 5 and up, it can be used as a liner or base for layering other glazes. It is food-safe and suitable for spray application. While primarily designed for traditional firings, artists have experimented successfully with wood, soda, and salt firings at higher temperatures.

glazes.name: Clementine
glazes.description: Clementine is an opaque, vibrant orange glaze that offers a consistent, reliable result suitable for classrooms. This glaze is food-safe and can be applied by spray or as a liner or base for layering other glazes. It works well for firing at cone 5 and above.

glazes.name: Lilac
glazes.description: Lilac is an opaque, light purple glaze with pooling and breaking effects on the surface, inspired by classic Fiesta tableware ceramics. Suitable for firing cone 5 and up, it can be used as a liner or base for layering other glazes. The color may lose intensity at higher temperatures.

glazes.name: Amethyst
glazes.description: Amethyst is an opaque, dark purple glaze with a rich, ancient appearance inspired by classic Fiesta tableware ceramics. It has a consistent, reliable result suitable for classrooms and can be used as a liner or base for layering other glazes. This glaze is food-safe and can achieve great results in wood, soda, and salt firings up to cone 12.

glazes.name: Turquoise
glazes.description: Turquoise is an intense, satin teal glaze producing attractive effects at mid-range temperatures and can be compared to ancient glazes. It is opaque and can achieve striking visual results with just one coat.

glazes.name: Coral
glazes.description: Coral is a vibrant persimmon-colored glaze producing intense color and attractive high-fire effects.

glazes.name: Red Gloss
glazes.description: Red Gloss is a glossy deep red glaze that produces intense color and attractive high-fire effects.

glazes.name: Snow Drift

images.alt: KI-11 over AMACO No.32 Dark Chocolate Clay, Cone 5

glazes.description: KI -11 Snow Drift is a diffused, milky white glaze. The Kiln Ice glazes are modeled after traditional Guan or Snowflake glazes and feature a delicate network of fine hairline cracks (known as crazing). They are translucent but become slightly opaque where the cracks form, creating a beautiful, multi-layered visual texture.

glazes.name: Arctic Blush

images.alt: KI-18 over AMACO No.32 Dark Chocolate Clay, Cone 5

glazes.description: KI-18 Arctic Blush is a pastel pink glaze with a delicate, milky quality. The Kiln Ice glazes feature a delicate network of fine hairline cracks (crazing), creating a beautiful, multi-layered visual texture that becomes slightly opaque where the cracks form.

glazes.name: Winter Glass

images.alt: KI-21 over AMACO No.32 Dark Chocolate Clay, Cone 5

glazes.description: KI-21 Winter Glass is a pastel, frosty blue featuring a delicate network of fine hairline cracks, creating a beautiful, multi-layered visual texture that becomes slightly opaque where the cracks form.

glazes.name: Glacial Lake

images.alt: KI-27 over AMACO No.32 Dark Chocolate Clay, Cone 5

glazes.description: KI-27 Glacial Lake is a vibrant blue-green hue. The glazes feature a delicate network of fine hairline cracks (crazing). They are translucent but become slightly opaque where the cracks form, creating a beautiful, multi-layered visual texture.

glazes.name: Frozen Fern

images.alt: KI-46 over AMACO No.32 Dark Chocolate Clay, Cone 5

glazes.description: Frozen Fern is a muddled yellow-green hue. The glaze features a delicate network of fine hairline cracks, creating a beautiful, multi-layered visual texture with translucency that becomes slightly opaque in certain areas.

glazes.name: Honey Crystal

images.alt: KI-68 over AMACO No.32 Dark Chocolate Clay, Cone 5

glazes.description: KI-68 Honey Crystal is a warm, golden, and buttery yellow color. These glazes feature a delicate network of fine hairline cracks (crazing) and are translucent but become slightly opaque where the cracks form, creating a beautiful, multi-layered visual texture.

glazes.name: Saturation Metallic
glazes.description: Satin metallic black glaze that resembles steel and other metals. Great as an accent glaze.

glazes.name: Saturation Gold
glazes.description: Saturation Gold has a smooth, satin/matte finish that is a dark gold similar to bronze when fired. Perfect for sculptural work and industrial ware, this glaze could be a unique accent for any work of art.

glazes.name: Palladium
glazes.description: This unique metallic glaze offers a mirrorlike finish when fired, enhancing any work with beautiful, shimmering accents. It requires three coats to achieve the smooth mirror finish. The fluid nature of the glaze necessitates careful firing to avoid overfiring. Ideal for low porosity bodies, it excels as an accent or when layered with other AMACO glazes, adding depth and visual interest.

glazes.name: Vintage Gold
glazes.description: Vintage Gold is an opaque metallic glaze developing small triangular gold crystals with dark haloes, creating a glittering patina effect. Not breaking over texture, this dramatic glaze is perfect for sculptural or non-functional work.

glazes.name: June Bug
glazes.description: June Bug is a glossy, dark green metallic glaze that develops small triangular iridescent crystals, similar to the wings of a beetle. This opaque glaze does not break over texture.

glazes.name: Blue Spark
glazes.description: Royal blue glaze with small triangular dark gray metallic crystals, resembling lapis lazuli, and an opaque surface that does not break over texture.

glazes.name: Blue Midnight
glazes.description: Blue Midnight creates glossy black with blue highlights where thick. Texture accentuates the effect.

glazes.name: Serpentine Green
glazes.description: Serpentine Green is a semi-matte soft green glaze that breaks light blue over texture. It is opaque and translucent, creating an ancient glaze effect.

glazes.name: Lapis Flow
glazes.description: Soft blue to purple with breaking green over texture. Opaque and glossy.

glazes.name: Satin Oribe
glazes.description: Satin Oribe adds a wonderful blue-green burnished look to your wares. The dark green pools and light grey breaks bring an extra level of depth to this opaque glaze.

glazes.name: Purple Crystal
glazes.description: Matte violet with glossier deep indigo tones and purple crystal floats.

glazes.name: Honey Flux
glazes.description: Ancient glazes shift from opaque honey and cream to a beautiful fluid white with honey flecks.

glazes.name: Tuscan Blue
glazes.description: Tuscan Blue is a deep blue, semi-matte glaze that breaks light blue to moss green over texture. It is opaque and translucent, creating an ancient glaze effect.

glazes.name: Moon River
glazes.description: PC-19 Moon River is a dreamy, flowy glaze shifting in color from a soft periwinkle to a milky blue. Opaque and glossy, it may create pooling and breaking effects. Similar to ancient glazes, this glaze offers a timeless appearance.

glazes.name: Blue Rutile
glazes.description: Blue Rutile is a flowing light blue glaze that breaks to brown where thinner, always interesting and opaque.

glazes.name: Arctic Blue
glazes.description: Arctic Blue is a beautiful, opaque light blue with a smooth, consistent surface that breaks tan over texture.

glazes.name: Blue Stone
glazes.description: Blue Stone features a smooth blue-green matte color with varying shades of green transitioning into dark blues on textured surfaces. The glaze appears opaque with an ancient glaze aesthetic.

glazes.name: Indigo Float
glazes.description: Indigo Float is an intensely blue glaze with a lighter blue surface float. This rich cobalt glaze breaks deep blue where thinner, creating an opaque appearance reminiscent of ancient glazes.

glazes.name: Sapphire Float
glazes.description: Sapphire Float is a glossy, denim-blue glaze with wispy white rutile float that breaks green-gray over texture, forming beautiful naturally flowing patterns.

glazes.name: Textured Turquoise
glazes.description: Subtle turquoise gloss, green-beige where thin, but beautiful aqua where thick. Fluid glaze.

glazes.name: Blue Lagoon
glazes.description: Cool tones of Blue Lagoon break milky green and pool into light blue shallows. A fluid glaze with interesting surface variations similar to tides in a blue lagoon.

glazes.name: Tourmaline
glazes.description: Tourmaline is an intense, opaque blue-green glaze that forms a smooth, strong surface with a crystalline visual effect, comparing artistically to ancient glazes.

glazes.name: Frosted Turquoise
glazes.description: Frosted Turquoise is an opaque gray-blue glaze that appears to crackle over deep blue, with a smooth, satiny surface. The glaze benefits from heavy application to achieve its color breaking effect.

glazes.name: Deep Olive Speckle
glazes.description: Rich, translucent, glossy green glaze with darker green speckles, opaque appearance with pooling and breaking effects, reminiscent of ancient glazes.

glazes.name: Temmoku
glazes.description: Temmoku is a textured, satin-matte dark brown/tan speckled glaze that looks great layered beneath some of the more fluid Potter's Choice glazes. With its consistent surface and reliable results, this glaze makes a fantastic liner for cups, pots, bowls, and other ware.

glazes.name: Oatmeal
glazes.description: Oatmeal is a toasty, light beige glaze that is relatively translucent and breaks clear over textured surfaces. It works well for layering with other Potters Choice glazes.

glazes.name: Albany Slip Brown
glazes.description: Albany Slip Brown is fluid and butter-colored where thick, becoming russet where thin. It works well when layered over or under other Potter's Choice glazes.

glazes.name: Iron Lustre
glazes.description: Brown where thin with blue-gray floats where thick. Opaque, crystalline appearance and creates ancient glaze effects.

glazes.name: Light Sepia
glazes.description: Light Sepia is an opalescent gray glaze that features strong variations on textured ware, breaking golden beige where thin and lending itself well to layering with other Potters Choice Glazes.

glazes.name: Ironstone
glazes.description: Ironstone is a glossy, dark glaze that is a deep chocolatey brown where thin and beige-bronze where thicker. It is opaque and mixes well with other Potter's Choice Glazes. The surface has an ancient glaze quality.

glazes.name: Iron Yellow
glazes.description: Glossy, soft yellow glaze with flecks of iron, relatively opaque and breaks clear over texture.

glazes.name: True Celadon
glazes.description: True Celadon is a delicate light green glaze that reproduces the effects of traditional Asian celadons. This glaze looks striking over any textured ware, offering great color response when layered with other Potter's Choice glazes. The surface finish is translucent and the glaze can create pooling and breaking effects.

glazes.name: Vert Lustre
glazes.description: Vert Lustre is a beautiful flowing green glaze with a metallic sheen like ancient Persian Lusterware, creating an opaque, glossy surface finish.

glazes.name: Seaweed
glazes.description: Seaweed is a deep green glaze that layers beautifully with other Potter's Choice glazes. It breaks into a brownish pattern over texture and becomes more fluid with thickness. Apply in multiple coats for full opacity.

glazes.name: Toasted Sage
glazes.description: Ancient glaze appearance, opaque warm gray with subtle frothy tones of pink and green.

glazes.name: Sage
glazes.description: Sage is an opaque, warm gray green glaze with a glossy surface finish that can create pooling effects. Similar in appearance to ancient glazes, it works well as a top coat with other Potter's Choice glazes and enhances the visual effects of pottery pieces.

glazes.name: Dark Green
glazes.description: Dark green glaze showcases textured and carved ware with an elegant, translucent finish.

glazes.name: Lustrous Jade
glazes.description: Lustrous jade is a glossy, opaque glaze that features a soft, even green finish similar to its namesake gemstone.

glazes.name: Emerald Falls
glazes.description: Emerald Falls is a beautiful glossy green glaze that breaks lighter and pools into a mauve grey-green. When applied in heavier coats, it creates interesting surface variations echoing mossy patches found in rolling emerald landscapes. This glaze is opaque and allows the glaze to float up, creating unique textures.

glazes.name: Art Deco Green
glazes.description: Art Deco Green is a glossy, light blue-green glaze with metallic spots floating to the surface, reminiscent of a patinated copper roof.

glazes.name: Frosted Melon
glazes.description: Frosted Melon has a massive, beige float that cracks to reveal green underneath while maintaining a smooth, satin surface. The subtle color variation due to iron fuming makes it beautiful on both smooth and textured ware. This glaze is opaque and can create interesting visual effects.

glazes.name: Deep Sienna Speckle
glazes.description: Deep Sienna Speckle is a warm, rich russet glaze that is translucent with dark speckles resembling the surface of reduction stoneware glazes.

glazes.name: Ancient Jasper
glazes.description: Ancient Jasper transitions from soft black to olive green to rust red as it is applied thicker. It has an active, varied surface by itself and layers with other glazes for striking results.

glazes.name: Chun Plum
glazes.description: Chun Plum is a pink-red translucent glaze that breaks light tan over textures, imitating the copper reds of ancient Asian ceramics with hints of pink and red in oxidation firings.

glazes.name: Ancient Copper
glazes.description: Ancient Copper is an iridescent iron red glaze that breaks dark brown over texture and pools dark yellow-green, giving it a weathered appearance. It has a more glossy appearance with metallic floats at cone 6.

glazes.name: Smokey Merlot
glazes.description: Smokey Merlot is a warm, dark purple glaze with a soft float of lilac. It creates beautiful visual effects like pooling and breaking on both smooth and textured surfaces and has an opaque appearance.

glazes.name: Deep Firebrick
glazes.description: Deep Firebrick is an opaque, brick red glaze that becomes translucent where thin. This bold, dark red glaze has an ancient glaze appearance and contains black specks.

glazes.name: Textured Amber
glazes.description: Semi-matte brown with varied, translucent surface and opaque tan speckles.

glazes.name: Textured Amber Brown
glazes.description: Textured Amber Brown is a glossy brown glaze that breaks lighter where thinner like a traditional temmoku glaze. This glaze layers well with other Potter's Choice glazes and produces fluid, beautiful effects.

glazes.name: Cosmic Tea Dust
glazes.description: Cosmic Tea Dust is a sparkly, dark brown glaze that breaks into a light yellow-brown over texture, shifting subtly from silver to copper with an iridescent quality.

glazes.name: Aventurine
glazes.description: Aventurine is an amber glaze with crystal flecks that break golden over texture and pool a darker shade in recessed areas. This glaze has an opaque surface finish.

glazes.name: Black Aventurine
glazes.description: Black Aventurine develops a metallic sparkle and can grow crystals if slowly cooled.

glazes.name: Cosmic Oilspot
glazes.description: Cosmic Oil Spot is a dark glaze with shimmery flecks creating streams and spots of color, reminiscent of traditional hare's fur glazes. The surface has an opaque appearance with visual effects similar to ancient glazes.

glazes.name: River Rock
glazes.description: Sandy, dark brown tones feather into a beautiful ebony before breaking into a rich amber when applied over texture. Subtle green-grey honeycomb shapes appear underneath the amber breaks on vertical surfaces. The glossy glaze has an opaque appearance with visual effects of pooling and breaking.

glazes.name: Golden Honey
glazes.description: Translucent warm yellow, Golden Honey breaks lighter on texture, a richer color where thick. Opaque and glossy, like whipped honey.

glazes.name: Cosmic Gold Dust

images.alt: PC-69 Cosmic Gold Dust Cone 6 Tile

glazes.description: Cosmic Gold Dust is a sparkly gold glaze with subtle specks of reds and browns in the light. Opaque and glossy, it may show pooling and breaking effects. Comparable to ancient glazes, this glaze can be used over AMACO Celadons, other Potter's Choice and Satin Matte glazes.

glazes.name: Copper Red
glazes.description: Oxblood red, opaque, this glaze creates ancient glaze effects with pooling and breaking surfaces.

glazes.name: Flambe
glazes.description: Copper red glaze with tones of purple and blue, breaking clear on edges or where thin. Opaque, glossy finish.

glazes.name: Fire & Ice
glazes.description: PC-72 Fire & Ice is deep red where thick and icy blue where thin. It features pooling effects, creating an ancient glaze appearance and is opaque. The glaze has a dramatic icy blue-colored break on texture.

glazes.name: Midnight Run
glazes.description: Midnight Run is a glossy dark blue-gray glaze like a velvety night sky, breaking dark across textured surfaces and beautiful on any color of clay.

glazes.name: Aurora Breeze
glazes.description: Aurora Breeze is a hazy pink glaze with hints of blue and purple and dark speckles. It develops a warm blush float when applied thickly on textured surfaces. Beautiful on any color of clay.

glazes.name: Honeydew
glazes.description: PCF-18 Honeydew is a glossy pale green glaze like the melon it's named for. It breaks across textured surfaces and develops a peachy float when thick. Beautiful on any color of clay. Good at cone 5, more fluid at cone 6.

glazes.name: Cirrus Flow
glazes.description: PCF-19 Cirrus Flow is a glossy pale blue glaze like a delicate cloud. This glaze breaks across textured surfaces and develops a peachy float when thick. Great for layering with other glazes as a base or top coat. Beautiful on any color of clay. Good at cone 5, more fluid at cone 6.

glazes.name: Ocean Drift
glazes.description: PCF-21 Ocean Drift is a calming blue color, interspersed with fine, dark speckles that add texture and visual interest. Great for layering with other glazes as a base or top coat. Beautiful on any color of clay. Opacity is achieved with multiple coats.

glazes.name: Mystic Tide
glazes.description: Mystic Tide is an earthy green with a subtle, mottled texture adding depth and complexity.Beautiful on any color of clay.

glazes.name: Rose Mist
glazes.description: Soft, blush-pink base with subtle hints of lavender and white swirls. Opaque and glossy finish.

glazes.name: Flux Blossom
glazes.description: PCF-55 Flux Blossom is a glossy pale pink glaze like a fresh flower. It breaks across textured surfaces and develops a nice peachy float when thick. Beautiful on any color of clay. Opaque and translucent.

glazes.name: Sunbeam
glazes.description: PCF-60 Sunbeam is a radiant golden-yellow with subtle gradations of amber and honey. Beautiful on any color of clay, it is opaque and great for layering as a base or top coat at cone 5 and 6. Shake well before use. Thin with water and AMACO Gum Solution if needed.

glazes.name: Sun Shower
glazes.description: PCF-63 Sun Shower is a pale yellow interspersed with fine, dark speckles. It breaks across textured surfaces and develops a warm blush float when thick. Opaque and great for layering as a base or top coat.

glazes.name: River Birch
glazes.description: PCF-74 River Birch is a glossy cream glaze with runny dark specks like the bark of a tree. It breaks across textured surfaces and develops a peachy float when thick. Great for layering with other glazes as a base or top coat. Beautiful on any color of clay. Very fluid at cone 6.

glazes.name: Moss Mist
glazes.description: PCF-75 Moss Mist is a glossy cream and green-mottled glaze that breaks across textured surfaces and develops a peachy float when thick. Beautiful on any color of clay. Very fluid at cone 6.

glazes.name: Night Rain
glazes.description: Deep indigo blue with lighter blue visual texture, PG-24 Night Rain is reminiscent of a night sky through a rain spattered window. Opaque and glossy, it may have pooling effects.

glazes.name: Teal Drift
glazes.description: A vivid turquoise with subtle spots where thick PG-42 Teal Drift is a rich, tropical color. Layers well over AMACO Celadons and Potter's Choice glazes. Apply 3-4 brush coats for best results. Let the glaze dry between coats.

glazes.name: Lunar Pink
glazes.description: A pale, cool pink with white visual texture where thick. Opaque and glossy.

glazes.name: Floating Lavender
glazes.description: Light purple with lighter visual spot texture where thick PG-55 Floating Lavender is light and airy as a cloud at sunset. Opaque, glossy finish.

glazes.name: Chai Gloss
glazes.description: Chai Gloss Shino produces varied shades of cream, orange, and brown with a glossy surface finish.

glazes.name: Chai Matte
glazes.description: Matte, varying shades of cream, orange, and brown.

glazes.name: Acai Gloss
glazes.description: Wondrously varied shades of blue and brown with a glossy surface finish.

glazes.name: Acai Matte
glazes.description: Acai Matte Shino yields wonderfully varied shades of blue and brown.

glazes.name: Cacao Gloss
glazes.description: Glossy, varied shades of brown and tan.

glazes.name: Cacao Matte
glazes.description: Cacao Matte Shino produces a range of brown and tan hues in a matte finish.

glazes.name: Oolong Gloss
glazes.description: Oolong Gloss Shino produces varied shades of mint green and brown.

glazes.name: Oolong Matte
glazes.description: Oolong Matte Shino produces varying shades of mint green and brown.

glazes.name: Matcha Gloss
glazes.description: Matcha Gloss Shino is a glossy, opaque glaze yielding wonderful shades of green and brown.

glazes.name: Matcha Matte
glazes.description: Matcha Matte Shino produces a range of green and brown shades with a matte surface finish.

glazes.name: Hibiscus Gloss
glazes.description: Glossy Hibiscus Gloss Shino yields wonderfully varied shades of salmon pink, orange, and brown.

glazes.name: Hibiscus Matte
glazes.description: Matte, yielding wonderfully varied shades of salmon, orange, and brown.

glazes.name: Black
glazes.description: Satin Matte Black is an opaque, buttery black glaze that accentuates textured ware and boasts a classic art deco appearance.

glazes.name: Stone
glazes.description: SM-2 Stone is a dark taupe satin glaze.

glazes.name: Marble
glazes.description: SM-3 Marble is a pale gray satin glaze, just a whisper of color like the polished marble it's inspired by.

glazes.name: Clear Satin
glazes.description: Clear Satin is a smooth, buttery glaze that accentuates textured ware and boasts a classic art deco appearance. Some underglaze colors may be muted.

glazes.name: White
glazes.description: Satin Matte White is an opaque, buttery white glaze that accentuates textured ware and features a classic art deco appearance.

glazes.name: Light Blue
glazes.description: Satin Matte Light Blue is an opaque, buttery robin's egg blue glaze that accentuates textured ware and boasts a classic art deco appearance.

glazes.name: Dark Blue
glazes.description: Opaque, buttery cobalt dark blue glaze with a satin matte finish, accentuating textured ware and offering a classic art deco appearance.

glazes.name: Seafoam
glazes.description: SM-24 Seafoam embodies the serene beauty of the ocean, bringing a sense of calm and sophistication to any pottery creation. This glaze offers a glossy finish with subtle pooling effects, evoking the tranquil look of ancient sea glazes. It is opaque and adds depth to the surface, enhancing the overall artistic appeal of your ceramic pieces.

glazes.name: Teal
glazes.description: Satin Matte Teal is an opaque, buttery blue-green glaze that accentuates textured ware and boasts a classic art deco appearance.

glazes.name: Blue Green
glazes.description: Opaque Satin Matte Blue Green glaze that accentuates textured ware and boasts a classic art deco appearance.

glazes.name: Mint
glazes.description: Opaque, buttery light green glaze that accentuates textured ware and boasts a classic art deco appearance.

glazes.name: Chartreuse
glazes.description: Satin Matte Chartreuse is an opaque, buttery pea-green glaze that accentuates textured ware and boasts a classic art deco appearance.

glazes.name: Moss
glazes.description: SM-46 Moss is a deep forest green, matte glaze with a hint of olive.

glazes.name: Red
glazes.description: Satin Matte Red is an opaque, buttery scarlet glaze that accentuates textured ware and boasts a classic art deco appearance.

glazes.name: Cayenne
glazes.description: SM-53 Cayenne is a spicy and warm red satin glaze with an earthy undertone. It has an opaque surface finish.

glazes.name: Goldenrod
glazes.description: SM-60 Goldenrod is a rich, deep yellow matte glaze like a summer flower.

glazes.name: Yellow
glazes.description: Satin Matte Yellow is an opaque, buttery gold glaze that accentuates textured ware and boasts a classic art deco appearance.

glazes.name: Orange
glazes.description: Satin Matte Orange is an opaque, buttery orange glaze that accentuates textured ware and boasts a classic art deco appearance.

glazes.name: Ivory Beige Underglaze
glazes.description: Ivory Beige Velvet Underglaze is a soft cream color that warms up slightly at cone 5. A clear glaze over Ivory Beige accentuates and darkens the color at cone 05 and cone 5, with no change in color at cone 10.

glazes.name: Terra Cotta Underglazes
glazes.description: Terra Cotta Velvet Underglaze is a warm cinnamon brown that darkens to a red-brown at cone 05 and becomes a deep chocolate color at cone 10. It is opaque and reminiscent of the clay it is named after, with a rich, velvety texture.

glazes.name: Straw Underglaze
glazes.description: Straw Velvet Underglaze is a rich sunflower yellow that maintains its color well at any temperature and becomes richer and darker under a clear glaze. It is opaque and can be applied in one coat for full coverage.

glazes.name: Yellow Underglaze
glazes.description: Yellow Velvet Underglaze is a bright lemon yellow that becomes pale at cone 10. A clear glaze over Yellow darkens the color slightly but has less effect at higher temperatures.

glazes.name: Deep Yellow Underglaze
glazes.description: Deep Yellow Velvet Underglaze is a rich ochre color that becomes less saturated and darker at higher temperatures. It is intense under a clear glaze and can be applied in one coat for opacity.

glazes.name: Nectar Underglaze
glazes.description: Nectar underglaze is a warm yellow color like honeysuckle flowers, becoming more light amber under a clear glaze. It slightly intensifies at cone 5 and cone 10, providing an ancient glazed appearance.

glazes.name: Red Brown Underglaze
glazes.description: Red Brown Velvet Underglaze is a warm cocoa color that darkens to reddish at cone 5 and becomes chocolatey at cone 10. A clear glaze deepens the color significantly at any temperature, creating an opaque finish.

glazes.name: Chocolate Brown Underglaze
glazes.description: Chocolate Brown Velvet Underglaze is a deep brown that darkens with higher cone temperatures. This underglaze appears stunning beneath multiple coats of clear glaze, creating a rich and warm appearance. The opacity increases with additional layers, enhancing the depth and intensity of the color.

glazes.name: Peach Underglaze
glazes.description: Peach Velvet Underglaze is a warm pink at cone 05 that darkens slightly at cone 5 and becomes a light coral at cone 10. A clear glaze darkens the color slightly at any temperature.

glazes.name: Coral Underglaze
glazes.description: Coral is a warm, bright pink underglaze that retains its color from cone 05 up to cone 10 with minimal darkening. Clear glaze brightens the color and can be used with zinc or non-zinc glazes without causing any color shift.

glazes.name: Rose Underglaze
glazes.description: Rose Velvet underglaze is a deep magenta that is dark at cone 05, becomes patchy at cone 5 before it burns out almost completely at cone 10. A clear glaze over Rose will accentuate the color at cone 05 but at cone 5 and 10, there is no color change between glazed and unglazed.

glazes.name: Lavender Underglaze
glazes.description: Lavender velvet underglaze is a soft periwinkle color that becomes deeper with higher firings. A clear glaze over Lavender enhances the color at lower cones and preserves bluish tones at higher cones. The clear glaze can be applied in one coat for opacity.

glazes.name: Lilac Underglaze
glazes.description: Lilac velvet underglaze is a soft plum color that becomes warmer and deeper with higher temperatures, starting to burn out at cone 10. A clear glaze over Lilac will accentuate the color at lower cones (cone 05 and 5) and reduce the intensity of the color at higher cones (cone 10).

glazes.name: Purple Underglaze
glazes.description: Purple Velvet underglaze is a violet color that becomes bluish and lighter at cone 5 before becoming very light and blue at cone 10. A clear glaze over Purple will accentuate the color at cone 05 and cone 5, causing more blue tones at cone 10.

glazes.name: Salmon Underglaze
glazes.description: Salmon Velvet underglaze is a soft peach color that lightens at cone 05, becomes warmer and darker at cone 5, and maintains a toasted peach color at cone 10. A clear glaze over Salmon will accentuate and darken the color at all cone temperatures.

glazes.name: Blush Underglaze
glazes.description: Blush is a warm, soft pink underglaze that retains color from cone 05 up to cone 10 with minimal darkening. Clear glaze brightens the color and can be used with zinc or non-zinc glazes without causing a color shift.

glazes.name: Baby Blue Underglaze
glazes.description: Baby Blue Velvet underglaze is a soft light blue color that is bright and pastel at cone 05, becomes darker at cone 5, and is darker sky blue at cone 10. A clear glaze over Baby Blue will accentuate the color at cone 05 but begins to burn out some of the color at cone 5 and cone 10.

glazes.name: Medium Blue Underglaze
glazes.description: Medium Blue Velvet Underglaze is a vivid primary blue that maintains its color across different temperatures, becoming darker at cone 5. A clear glaze over Medium Blue will significantly darken the color. This underglaze has an opaque finish and can be applied in one coat for full opacity.

glazes.name: Turquoise Blue Underglaze
glazes.description: Turquoise Blue Velvet underglaze is a blue green color that is cyan at cone 05, becomes deeper cone 5, and becomes very dark at cone 10. This underglaze maintains its color well under a clear glaze at any temperature.

glazes.name: Iceberg Blue Underglaze
glazes.description: Iceberg Blue Velvet underglaze is a light blue color that is powder blue at cone 05, becomes slightly darker at cone 5, and maintains its light blue shade up to cone 10. A clear glaze over Iceberg Blue tends to lift the color out slightly, lightening it.

glazes.name: Sea Glass Underglaze
glazes.description: Sea Glass is a very pale blue-green color that becomes richer and darker under a clear glaze. It remains the same at cone 5 but fired to cone 10 it becomes more blue. This glaze has an opaque surface finish.

glazes.name: Indigo Underglaze
glazes.description: Indigo is a deep purple-blue color that darkens under a clear glaze. When fired to cone 5, it becomes more blue and slightly darker at cone 10.

glazes.name: Teal Blue Underglaze
glazes.description: Teal Blue underglaze is a deep blue color that becomes rich sapphire at cone 05 and a deep medium blue at cone 5. The color darkens significantly when clear glaze is applied over it, regardless of the firing temperature.

glazes.name: Avocado Underglaze
glazes.description: Avocado Velvet Underglaze is a vivid pea green at cone 05, darkens to a deep chartreuse at cone 10. A clear glaze significantly deepens the color. We recommend using HF-9 Zinc Free Clear with this underglaze for optimal results. Most underglazes and coloring oxides will show their best color with Zinc-Free Clear.

glazes.name: Royal Blue Underglaze
glazes.description: Royal Blue Velvet Underglaze is a deep and saturated navy blue that darkens and nearly becomes black as the firing temperature increases. A clear glaze significantly deepens the color at any temperature, making it ideal for creating rich, vibrant blue surfaces with an almost black tone at higher temperatures.

glazes.name: Blue Green Underglaze
glazes.description: Blue Green Velvet Underglaze is a teal color that deepens to dark marine blue with increasing cone temperatures. A clear glaze significantly enhances the color intensity at any temperature. The opaque underglaze creates a rich, deep aqua finish at cone 05 and becomes darker as the firing temperature increases.

glazes.name: Chartreuse Underglaze
glazes.description: Chartreuse Velvet Underglaze is a bright lime green at cone 05, softens to a mint green at cone 5, and darkens slightly at cone 10. A clear glaze accentuates the color significantly at any temperature. The underglaze offers an opaque appearance and can be applied in one coat for full opacity.

glazes.name: Light Green Underglaze
glazes.description: Light Green Velvet Underglaze is a bright spring green at cone 05, darkens significantly to forest green at cone 5, and becomes a deep pine green at cone 10. A clear glaze accentuates the color at any temperature. The color is opaque and provides vibrant visual effects.

glazes.name: Pistachio Underglaze
glazes.description: Pistachio is a light green underglaze that retains its color from cone 05 up to cone 6 with minimal darkening. Above cone 6 the color becomes slightly bluer. Clear glaze brightens the color.

glazes.name: Cactus Underglaze
glazes.description: Cactus is a cool light green underglaze that retains color from cone 05 up to cone 6 with minimal darkening. Above cone 6 the color becomes slightly bluer. Clear glaze brightens the color.

glazes.name: Orange Underglaze
glazes.description: Orange Velvet Underglaze is a golden color that softens to a toasty beige and becomes toastier with higher temperatures. A clear glaze accentuates the color significantly at any temperature. The clear glaze makes the underglaze appear more vibrant.

glazes.name: Dark Green Underglaze
glazes.description: Dark Green Velvet Underglaze is a rich green color that becomes more saturated at cone 5 and slightly darker at cone 10. A clear glaze deepens the color significantly. We recommend using HF-9 Zinc Free Clear for this underglaze to ensure color consistency.

glazes.name: Pearl Gray Underglaze
glazes.description: Pearl Gray Velvet Underglaze is a bluish silver gray color that darkens to a pewter tone with higher cone temperatures. A clear glaze enhances the color significantly at any temperature. The surface finish is opaque and smooth, providing a rich, ancient glaze appearance.

glazes.name: Ultra White Underglaze
glazes.description: Ultra White Velvet Underglaze is a bright and opaque white at cone 05, a frosty color at cone 5, and maintains its vividness at cone 10. A clear glaze accentuates the color of the underglaze significantly at any temperature.

glazes.name: White Underglaze
glazes.description: White Velvet Underglaze is snow white color that is bright and minty at cone 05, softens at cone 5, and becomes slight warmer, but maintains a clean white color at cone 10. A clear glaze accentuates the underglaze at any temperature.

glazes.name: Jet Black Underglaze
glazes.description: Jet Black Velvet Underglaze is a cool black color that is smooth and velvety at cone 05, darkens at cone 5, and becomes darkest black and slightly glossy at cone 10. A clear glaze deepens and accentuates the underglaze significantly at any temperature. Use 2 coats over underglazes to show the color.

glazes.name: Ultra Black Underglaze
glazes.description: Ultra Black Velvet Underglaze fires to a beautiful soft, dark matte color at cone 05 then deepens to a darker black at cone 5 and maintains its shade at cone 10. A clear glaze accentuates the color significantly at any temperature. Use 2 coats over underglazes to show the color fully.

glazes.name: Morning Fog Underglaze
glazes.description: Morning Fog Velvet Underglaze fires to a light, misty gray when fired to cone 05, darkens to a steamy color at cone 5, and maintains the foggy color at cone 10. A clear glaze accentuates the color of the underglaze significantly at any temperature.

glazes.name: Elephant Underglaze
glazes.description: Elephant Velvet Underglaze is a light, ashy shade when fired to cone 05, darkens to a deeper gray at cone 5 and turns to a grayish-black akin to elephant skin when fired to cone 10. A clear glaze accentuates the color of the underglaze significantly at any temperature.

glazes.name: Slate Underglaze
glazes.description: Slate Velvet Underglaze is a dark, bluish-gray at cone 05 that darkens to a deep, slatey shade when fired to cone 5 and transforms into a melancholic blue-green at cone 10. A clear glaze accentuates the color of the underglaze significantly at any temperature.

glazes.name: Teddy Bear Brown
glazes.description: Teddy Bear Velvet Underglaze is a warm tan at cone 05, darkens to a russet color at cone 5, and lightens and begins to burn out at cone 10. A clear glaze accentuates the underglaze at cone 05 but burns out some of the color at cone 5 and cone 10. We recommend using HF-9 Zinc Free Clear for this underglaze to maintain color integrity. Most underglazes, stains, and coloring oxides will show their best color with Zinc-Free Clear.

glazes.name: Velour Black Underglaze
glazes.description: Velour Black Velvet Underglaze is a warm black color that is deep and velvety at cone 05, darkens and becomes warmer at cone 5, and is silky and deep at cone 10. A clear glaze deepens the color of the underglaze significantly at any temperature. Use 2 coats over underglazes to show the color.

glazes.name: Maroon Underglaze
glazes.description: Maroon Velvet Underglaze is a deep burgundy color that is soft and rich at cone 05, deepens at cone 5, and begins to burn out at cone 10. A clear glaze deepens the color of the underglaze significantly at cone 05 and intensifies its rich oxblood shade at cone 5.

glazes.name: Hunter Green Underglaze
glazes.description: Hunter Green Velvet Underglaze is an emerald color that vividly stands out at cone 05, darkens to a deep blue-green at cone 5, and becomes slightly darker with a sheen at cone 10. A clear glaze significantly deepens the color of the underglaze at any temperature. We recommend using HF-9 Zinc Free Clear for best results. Most underglazes, stains, and coloring oxides show their best color with Zinc-Free Clear.

glazes.name: Ultra Violet Underglaze
glazes.description: Ultra Violet Velvet Underglaze is a soft, pinkish-purple at cone 05, darkens slightly at cone 5, then lightens when fired to cone 10 but still maintains its violet shade. A clear glaze accentuates the color of the underglaze significantly at any temperature.

glazes.name: Violet Underglaze
glazes.description: Violet Velvet Underglaze is a light plum color that softens at cone 05, darkens at cone 5, and becomes a deep lilac color with some burnout at cone 10. A clear glaze enhances the underglaze color effectively across all temperatures. This underglaze offers a soft transition in hues depending on the firing temperature.

glazes.name: Amethyst Underglaze
glazes.description: Amethyst Velvet Underglaze is a deep grape color that becomes a deep violet and slightly loses its intensity at cone 10. A clear glaze significantly deepens the color at any temperature, creating an opaque finish. The surface has a glossy appearance with subtle crystalline effects.

glazes.name: Red Underglaze
glazes.description: Red Velvet Underglaze is a vivid crimson at cone 05, deepens to a dark wine red at cone 5, and becomes brownish and begins to burn out at cone 10. A clear glaze accentuates the color nicely at these cones.

glazes.name: Light Red Underglaze
glazes.description: Light Red Velvet Underglaze is a bright coral red that softens slightly and darkens at higher temperatures becoming glossy at cone 10. A clear glaze accentuates the color nicely at any temperature. The color becomes darker and slightly glossy at cone 10, with a clear glaze enhancing its appearance.

glazes.name: Real Orange Underglaze
glazes.description: Real Orange Velvet Underglaze is a luminous orange color that is bright and saturated at cone 05, softens and becomes lighter at cone 5 and becomes slightly warmer and is still bright at cone 10. A clear glaze accentuates the color of the underglaze significantly at any temperature.

glazes.name: Cinnamon Underglaze
glazes.description: Cinnamon Velvet Underglaze is a deep sienna color that becomes chocolatey at cone 5 and dark brown at cone 10. A clear glaze significantly deepens the color at any temperature, creating a rich, warm appearance.

glazes.name: Electric Blue Underglaze
glazes.description: Electric Blue Velvet Underglaze is a very deep cobalt blue that vividly stands out at cone 05, darkens significantly at cone 5, and becomes a very dark navy blue at cone 10. A clear glaze deepens the color of the underglaze significantly at any temperature.

glazes.name: Bright Red Underglaze
glazes.description: Bright Red Velvet Underglaze is a deep crimson color that vividly highlights at cone 05, darkens slightly at cone 5, and becomes a deep cherry red at cone 10. A clear glaze accentuates the underglaze significantly at any temperature. We recommend using HF-10 Clear for best results.

glazes.name: Radiant Red Underglaze
glazes.description: Radiant Red Velvet Underglaze is a bright scarlet color that deepens and becomes slightly glossy and a deep fire-engine red at cone 10. A clear glaze accentuates the underglaze significantly at any temperature. We recommend using HF-10 Clear for Radiant Red Velvet Underglaze, with HF-9 Zinc-Free Clear recommended for use with underglazes to reduce color shift.

glazes.name: Flame Orange Underglaze
glazes.description: Flame Orange Velvet Underglaze is a bright red-orange at cone 05, darkens to a deep persimmon color at cone 10. A clear glaze accentuates the underglaze significantly at any temperature. We recommend using HF-9 Zinc-Free Clear for best results with underglazes to reduce color shift.

glazes.name: Bright Orange Underglaze
glazes.description: Bright Orange Velvet Underglaze is a tangerine color that neon at cone 05, darkens slightly at cone 5 and becomes a deep pumpkin at cone 10. A clear glaze accentuates the underglaze significantly at any temperature. We recommend using HF-10 Clear for this underglaze.

glazes.name: Intense Yellow Underglaze
glazes.description: Intense Yellow Velvet Underglaze is a vivid pineapple color at cone 05, deepens to a deeper and most intense hue at cone 10. A clear glaze significantly enhances the color depth at any temperature. For best results, use HF-9 Zinc-Free Clear or HF-10 Clear.

glazes.name: Blood Orange Underglaze
glazes.description: Blood Orange is a fiery tangerine color that intensifies under a clear glaze when fired to Cone 5 and Cone 10, becoming slightly darker. The glaze has an opaque surface finish.

glazes.name: Ebony
glazes.description: DL-1 Ebony is an opaque glossy black glaze, great as a base for layering or on its own.

glazes.name: Enamel White
glazes.description: DL-11 Enamel White is a gloss white glaze, great as a liner or on its own. It can also serve as a base for layering.

glazes.name: Midnight Blue
glazes.description: DL-12 Midnight Blue is a glossy, deep navy blue glaze that breaks into lighter blue highlights where thick. This glaze is good for textured work and layers well with other DL glazes.

glazes.name: Floating Blue
glazes.description: DL-20 Floating Blue is a classic blue glaze that appears pale where thick and darker blue where thinner, breaking into iron brown specks.

glazes.name: Ultramarine

images.alt: Glaze sample chip of DL-23 Ultramarine

glazes.description: DL-23 Ultramarine is a glossy cobalt blue glaze with a lighter float. Moves when fired and breaks gray where thin. Beautiful for layering or alone.

glazes.name: Coastline
glazes.description: DL-25 Coastline is a glossy turquoise glaze that transitions to a green-beige hue where thin, adding depth to textured pieces and layers well with other DL glazes.

glazes.name: Storm Cloud
glazes.description: Deep navy glaze with pooling and accentuated texture. Beautiful on its own or layered for an oil spot effect.

glazes.name: Low Tide
glazes.description: DL-33 Low Tide is a glossy iron glaze, transparent brown where thin and gray-green where thick. Great as a base coat for dramatic layering glazes or alone, highlights texture.

glazes.name: River Moss
glazes.description: DL-42 River Moss is a glossy deep green opaque glaze that breaks clear where thin. Some movement when fired, beautiful as a layering top coat or alone.

glazes.name: Brick Red
glazes.description: DL-59 Brick Red is an opaque vivid red speckled glaze that breaks translucent where thin. This bold color is great on its own or layered with other DL glazes. While measuring the specific gravity is not required to initially mix the AMACO Dipping and Layering glazes, there may be a time you need to know the recommended specific gravity for DL-59 Brick Red is 1.58. MIXING DIRECTIONS: Wear a NIOSH approved mask for dust. Pour 1 gallon (3.8L) of distilled water into a 2 gallon (7.6L) or larger bucket. Avoid breathing dust while adding product to water. Mix contents until smooth and free of lumps. For best results, sieve glaze through 80-mesh screen and let sit overnight. Please refer to detailed instructions inside box. glazes.name: Honeycomb
glazes.description: DL-60 Honeycomb is a satiny golden-yellow glaze with a darker texture where thicker. Some movement when fired. Lovely as an accent or base for layering.

glazes.name: Stardust
glazes.description: DL-63 Stardust is a dark brown and black glaze with sparkles that shift between silver and copper. This glaze works well as a base or top layer when paired with other DL glazes, creating an ancient glaze effect. It is opaque and can be used for both aesthetic and functional pottery.

glazes.name: Oxblood
glazes.description: DL-70 Oxblood develops a rich Oxblood red with thick application. It breaks clear and may run during firing. The surface finish is glossy.

glazes.name: Kings Blue
glazes.description: Kings Blue will fire matte without addition of a clear glaze. To intensify color or for use on dinnerware, apply clear or matte clear glaze. Color concentration occurs. Recommended clear glaze is SW-001 Stoneware Clear.

glazes.name: Crimson
glazes.description: Crimson will fire matte without addition of a clear glaze. To intensify color or for use on dinnerware, apply clear or matte clear glaze. Color fades. Recommended clear glaze is SW-001 Stoneware Clear.

glazes.name: Pink Pink
glazes.description: Pink will fire matte without addition of a clear glaze. To intensify color or for use on dinnerware, apply clear or matte clear glaze. Color concentration occurs. Recommended clear glaze is SW-001 Stoneware Clear.

glazes.name: Electra Blue
glazes.description: Electra Blue will fire matte without addition of a clear glaze. To intensify color or for use on dinnerware, apply clear or matte clear glaze. Color concentration occurs. Recommended clear glaze is SW-001 Stoneware Clear.

glazes.description: Speckled Moody Blue is an opaque, heavily pigmented stroke & coat® glaze with white specks. One coat creates a translucent finish; additional coats increase opacity. The color lightens to medium brown upon firing, with specks remaining. It has an ancient glazed appearance.

glazes.name: Speckled Grapel
glazes.description: Speckled Grapel is a heavily pigmented, viscous Stroke & Coat® glaze with blue specks. One coat creates a translucent finish; two to three coats are suggested for full opacity and complete coverage. It fires to a glossy finish without clear glaze. The color slightly lightens upon firing, and the specks bleed.

glazes.name: Speckled Tuxedo
glazes.description: One coat creates a translucent finish, while additional coats increase opacity. Fire to a glossy finish. Speckled Tuxedo has an opaque, heavily pigmented appearance with white specks.

glazes.name: Speckled Cotton Tail
glazes.description: One coat creates a translucent finish, while two to three coats add opacity and cover fully. Speckled Cotton Tail features yellow, orange, and blue specks that blend subtly.

glazes.name: Speckled Green Thumb
glazes.description: Speckled Green Thumb is a heavily pigmented, viscous glaze with black specks that creates a translucent finish, becoming more opaque with additional coats.

glazes.name: Speckled Sour Apple
glazes.description: Speckled Sour Apple is a heavily pigmented, viscous Stroke & Coat® glaze with blue specks that can be used for brush strokes or opaque coverage. One coat will create a translucent finish with subsequent coats adding opacity. We suggest two to three coats for full opacity and all over coverage. Will fire to a gloss finish without clear glaze. Opaque. No color change.

glazes.name: Speckled The Blues
glazes.description: Speckled The Blues is an opaque, heavily pigmented glaze with blue specks, creating an ancient glaze effect. It has a glossy finish.

glazes.name: Speckled My Blue Heaven
glazes.description: Speckled My Blue Heaven is a heavily pigmented, viscous Stroke & Coat® glaze with blue specks that can be used for brush strokes or opaque coverage. One coat will create a translucent finish with subsequent coats adding opacity. We suggest two to three coats for full opacity and all over coverage. Will fire to a gloss finish without clear glaze.

glazes.name: Speckled Purple Haze
glazes.description: Speckled Purple Haze is a heavily pigmented, viscous glaze with blue specks that can be used for brush strokes or opaque coverage. One coat will create a translucent finish; subsequent coats add opacity until full opacity is reached. Fires to a glossy finish without the need for clear glaze. Color slightly lightens during firing and specks bleed.

glazes.name: Speckled Vanilla Dip
glazes.description: Speckled Vanilla Glaze is heavily pigmented with orange, brown, and black specks, creating an opaque, glossy finish. The glaze has an ancient glaze appearance with visible pooling and breaking effects.

glazes.name: Speckled Silver Lining
glazes.description: Speckled Silver Lining is heavily pigmented with red and black specks, creating an opaque coverage. One coat produces a translucent finish; additional coats increase opacity. Upon firing, the color transforms to medium blue, with specks blurring and bleeding. The glaze results in a glossy finish.

glazes.name: Speckled Pink-A-Dot
glazes.description: Speckled Pink-A-Dot creates a translucent finish with subsequent coats adding opacity, suggesting two to three coats for full coverage and opacity. Fires to a glossy finish without clear glaze. Specks bleed.

glazes.name: Speckled Hot Tamale
glazes.description: Speckled Hot Tamale is a heavily pigmented, viscous Stroke & Coat® glaze with white specks that can be used for brush strokes or opaque coverage. One coat will create a translucent finish with subsequent coats adding opacity. We suggest two to three coats for full opacity and all over coverage. Will fire to a gloss finish without clear glaze.

glazes.name: Speckled Orange-A-Peel
glazes.description: Speckled Orange-A-Peel creates a translucent finish, with two to three coats recommended for full opacity and complete coverage. It fires to a glossy finish without the need for a clear glaze. The color slightly lightens upon firing, and specks bleed.

glazes.name: Speckled Tu Tu Tango
glazes.description: Speckled Tu Tu Tango is a heavily pigmented, viscous Stroke & Coat® glaze with yellow, blue and green specks that can be used for brush strokes or opaque coverage. One coat will create a translucent finish; two to three coats are suggested for full opacity and all over coverage. It will fire to a gloss finish without clear glaze.

glazes.name: Stoneware Crackle Matte Clear
glazes.description: Crackle Matte Clear is a soft matte, semi-transparent glaze that develops a fine crackle pattern. A minimum of two coats are needed to develop a crackle pattern. To highlight the crackle pattern, after firing, wipe black acrylic or ink over the ware and wipe off, allowing the ink to fill the cracks.

glazes.description: Stoned Denim is a variegated blue glaze that breaks to brown.

glazes.name: Black Walnut

images.alt: Over SW-401 Light Flux (left) and SW-402 Dark Flux (right), cone 6 oxidation
Under SW-401 Light Flux (left) and SW-402 Dark Flux (right), cone 6 oxidation
Under SW-401 Light Flux (left) and SW-402 Dark Flux (right), cone 10 reduction

glazes.description: Black Walnut is a dark brown soft matte glaze that breaks to a rich warm brown. Rich brown undertones come through. The thinner the application, the lighter brown the color; the thicker the application, the darker the brown color. For a saturated opaque finish, apply 4+ coats. Has a satin finish.

glazes.name: Frost Blue

Over SW-401 Light Flux (left) and SW-402 Dark Flux (right),
cone 6 oxidation

glazes.description: Frost Blue is a mottled blue soft matte glaze that breaks into muted green/gray. It can exhibit semi-gloss finish with variegated blue/light blue and green hues.

glazes.name: Alabaster

Under SW-401 Light Flux (left) and SW-402 Dark Flux (right), cone 6 oxidation

glazes.description: Alabaster is a semi-opaque white soft matte glaze that breaks over texture. It has a satin finish and combines well with 2 coats of stroke and coat on top. Wood and soda firing results will vary.

glazes.name: Dunes
glazes.description: Semi-opaque off-white soft matte glaze that breaks over texture. The lighter the application, the more brown the glaze will appear; the thicker the application, the lighter tan the color.

glazes.name: Green Tea
glazes.description: Green Tea is a variegated sage green glaze that breaks over texture, producing a glossy blue finish. Thicker application will produce more crystals. This glaze combos well with other Mayco glazes.

glazes.name: Capri Blue
glazes.description: Variegated blue/green glaze with breaking effect over texture. Semi-gloss finish. Opaque.

glazes.name: Oyster

images.alt:
Over SW-401 Light Flux (left) and SW-402 Dark Flux (right),
cone 6 oxidation
Under SW-401 Light Flux (left) and SW-402 Dark Flux (right), cone 6 oxidation
Under SW-401 Light Flux (left) and SW-402 Dark Flux (right), cone 10 reduction

glazes.description: Oyster is a variegated cream/brown glaze with gray/purple undertones and breaks brown. An iridescent finish may be achieved by re-firing or if your kiln fires hot. If you like Green Tea and Capri Blue combos, then try Oyster because these three glazes are in the same glaze family.

glazes.name: Wrought Iron

images.alt:
Over SW-401 Light Flux (left) and SW-402 Dark Flux (right),
cone 6 oxidation
Under SW-401 Light Flux (left) and SW-402 Dark Flux (right), cone 6 oxidation
Under SW-401 Light Flux (left) and SW-402 Dark Flux (right), cone 10 reduction

glazes.description: Wrought Iron creates an intermittent, dappled black glossy-matte finish that is reminiscent of ancient oil-spot glazes. The thicker the application, the more black the glaze; the thinner the application, the more brown the glaze. This glaze has a matte finish with variation of gloss due to its high manganese content and may show bubbling on speckled clay bodies.

glazes.name: Tiger’s Eye

images.alt:
Over SW-401 Light Flux (left) and SW-402 Dark Flux (right),
cone 6 oxidation
Under SW-401 Light Flux (left) and SW-402 Dark Flux (right), cone 6 oxidation
Under SW-401 Light Flux (left) and SW-402 Dark Flux (right), cone 10 reduction

glazes.description: Tiger’s Eye breaks from a glossy deep brownish-red to frothy amber depending on the application thickness. One to two coats will produce browns and reds; three to five coats will result in more glaze movement and color variation. Thinner applications will produce darker browns and reds; heavier applications will lean towards an opaque, light brown finish. Butted against another glaze, Tiger’s Eye will gently merge with it—SW-104 Black Walnut is a nice complementary glaze to use. Wood and soda firing results will vary.

glazes.name: Speckled Plum

images.alt:
Over SW-401 Light Flux (left) and SW-402 Dark Flux (right),
cone 6 oxidation
Under SW-401 Light Flux (left) and SW-402 Dark Flux (right), cone 6 oxidation
Under SW-401 Light Flux (left) and SW-402 Dark Flux (right), cone 10 reduction

glazes.description: Speckled Plum produces a reddish brown gloss finish with one to two light coats and evolves to deliver floating maroon-mauve tones with heavier applications of three to four coats. Heavier applications may also yield bands of reddish-mauve specks when used with certain clay bodies. Thinner applications will produce darker browns and reds, while heavier applications will begin to show an opaque, maroon-mauve float.

glazes.name: Midnight Rain

images.alt:
Over SW-401 Light Flux (left) and SW-402 Dark Flux (right),
cone 6 oxidation
Under SW-401 Light Flux (left) and SW-402 Dark Flux (right), cone 6 oxidation
Under SW-401 Light Flux (left) and SW-402 Dark Flux (right), cone 10 reduction

glazes.description: Midnight Rain is a blue-green breaking gloss glaze with black crystals that create oil-like streaks, darkening the color and increasing movement. The base glaze SW-100 Blue Surf creates a lighter crystal effect when applied with a third coat.

glazes.name: Robin’s Egg

glazes.description: Robin’s Egg is a greenish-blue matte glaze that contains a sand-colored crystal. A small sandy-brown bloom appears as the crystal melts, creating a mottled, textured finish. The glaze has a semi-gloss surface with variegated blue and light blue hues and crystals.

glazes.name: Honeycomb

Under SW-401 Light Flux (left) and SW-402 Dark Flux (right), cone 10 reduction

glazes.description: Honeycomb is a variegated brown matte glaze that contains a sandy-brown crystal. As the crystal melts, it creates glossy blooms producing yellow mottling and a textured fired finish. For a lighter crystal effect, apply two coats of SW-103 Sisal with a third coat of SW-117 Honeycomb.

glazes.name: Sea Salt
glazes.description: Sea Salt is a white matte glaze that contains a sage green crystal. As the crystal melts, green blooms appear in the glaze, producing a foamy, textured fired finish.

glazes.name: Cinnabar

glazes.description: Cinnabar is a rich maroon-red gloss glaze that tends to develop a hazy purple float with heavier application. You'll produce beautiful color variations with Cinnabar.

glazes.name: Northern Woods

images.alt: Under SW-401 Light Flux (left) and SW-402 Dark Flux (right), cone 6 oxidation

Over SW-401 Light Flux (left) and SW-402 Dark Flux (right),
cone 6 oxidation
Under SW-401 Light Flux (left) and SW-402 Dark Flux (right), cone 10 reduction

glazes.description: Northern Woods is a semi-transparent bluish-green glaze. Lighter applications produce a light translucent green with brown hues, while additional coats deepen the green hues and add a bluish tint. It looks very nice on white stoneware and porcelain. When applied thin, the color appears brown and breaks brown. Apply three heavy coats for the deep green color.

glazes.name: Smoke

glazes.description: Smoke is an opaque grey-blue stoneware glaze with soft, subtle variegating hues that change to a variegated sage green with a glossy finish during firing. Brown breaks on raised surfaces on the body.

glazes.name: Maycoshino

images.alt:
Over SW-401 Light Flux (left) and SW-402 Dark Flux (right),
cone 6 oxidation
Under SW-401 Light Flux (left) and SW-402 Dark Flux (right), cone 6 oxidation
Under SW-401 Light Flux (left) and SW-402 Dark Flux (right), cone 10 reduction

glazes.description: Maycoshino is a variegating iron glaze with subtle color changes. In a thin application, it achieves rich, iron browns. Applied thicker, it produces lighter, creamy hues reminiscent of traditional Japanese Shino glazes. This glaze creates a solid reddish brown opaque covering with one coat; two coats result in a cream-tan-buff finish that breaks brown over surface texture; three coats introduce waves of cream-tan color over the reddish brown base.

glazes.name: Sapphire

glazes.description: Sapphire is a rich, deep cobalt blue glaze with a glossy, mirrored surface that can give colors ranging from dark blue to bluish-purple, depending on the thickness of application.

glazes.name: Olivine
glazes.description: Olivine has rusts and several shades of green flow from this mobile gloss glaze. Lighter coats produce muted greens and browns, while 3 coats can produce a beautiful metallic green, almost mirror-like. Color flattens and warm undertones come through.

glazes.name: Cordovan

images.alt: Under SW-401 Light Flux (left) and SW-402 Dark Flux (right), cone 10 reduction

glazes.description: Cordovan develops a deep, burgundy red color with a semi-gloss surface when fired to cone 5 or 6. Two coats are recommended for deep red, opaque coverage; light glazes may require a third coat.

glazes.name: Copper Float

images.alt:
Over SW-401 Light Flux (left) and SW-402 Dark Flux (right),
cone 6 oxidation
Under SW-401 Light Flux (left) and SW-402 Dark Flux (right), cone 6 oxidation
Under SW-401 Light Flux (left) and SW-402 Dark Flux (right), cone 10 reduction

glazes.description: Copper Float glaze is a rich and complex midrange glaze with hues varying from reds to coppers to gun metal blacks. The predominant coloration is a dark rusty red. The surface is satin-matte when fired to cone 5 or 6. Finishes flatten at higher temperatures, resulting in warm brown tones with some copper mottling. Thicker coats can produce metallic red hues. Wood and soda firing results will vary.

glazes.name: Copper Jade
glazes.description: Copper Jade fires to a glossy finish, producing colors that range from brown-greens to creamy tan hues. Color breaks dark and can pool into red tones where thick. This glaze yields complex surfaces through movement and color variation. Color changes to a red/brown with some black/copper mottling.

glazes.name: Birch
